entry.title: Managing Threats and Security Incidents for SAP Systems
entry.content:
By Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

The scale and impact of cyber attacks on companies and government institutions is increasing continuously. Protecting and responding to threats and attacks are therefore key components of defending critical business operations these days.

SAP strives to provide customers with secure software and services through the Secure Development and Operations Lifecycle (SDOL) and provides security guides and configuration maps. Meanwhile, penetration tests and issues found by external security researchers lead to product improvements and security patches. SAP also runs a bug bounty program and secure vulnerability disclosure program that helps us in continuously uplifting and improving our products and recognizes outside contributors. But that is only the start in running production SAP systems securely and manage the threats and security incidents they may be subjected to.

 

Secure Operations and Monitoring

When customers run SAP systems on premise, they are responsible for the secure configuration and operations of the solution, including the entire technical stack it runs on:  servers, databases and networking infrastructure. That also includes threat detection and security incident response for the full stack. For organizations  running SAP solutions on premise, attacks on enterprise IT and corporate credentials can spill over into SAP systems. For instance, if a customer experienced a ransomware incident in their own enterprise infrastructure any SAP systems hosted there could also be impacted by the event.

In addition, managing threats against SAP systems requires monitoring the secure configuration of the solutions themselves, as well as secure configuration of the business processes the solution supports. Threats include:

  • malicious use of privileges and authorizations
  • deployment of malicious custom code and hidden batch jobs
  • exploitation of known vulnerabilities in systems running behind in security patches.

Shared Responsibility Model and Privacy Boundaries in the Cloud

Many customers choose to move to the cloud for security and compliance reasons. However, even when using cloud services, key responsibilities remain on the customer end for threat detection and incident response. The Shared Responsibility Model for security doesn’t only separate responsibilities, it also established strict privacy boundaries. This model is illustrated in the diagram below. You can also find a variant in this white paper specifically for RISE with SAP Private Edition.

SAP protects, monitors and responds to threats in cloud infrastructure and control plane, networks and server infrastructure, data stores and cloud operations. For SaaS cloud solutions, that also includes deployment of SAP security patches before they are released on Patch Tuesday every month.

But SAP has no access to the secure configuration and transactions of customer applications. Customers provision users and authorizations, the configuration of business processes, and who has access to what data and functionality in the system. SAP doesn’t have user access to customer systems, outside of support cases, for instance, where customers grant the users access into their system. Therefore, SAP can’t monitor or respond to threats at those levels within the application on behalf of customers.

 

Threat Detection for SAP Solutions

Many cyber threats target core IT infrastructure, rather than specialized applications like ERP systems. Research published by Onapsis and Flashpoint in August 2024, though, indicates a rising threat of malware specifically targeting SAP solutions – rather than the underlying operating system, for instance. They also report a dramatic rise in chatter by adversaries discussing SAP vulnerabilities and exploits. Whether running on-premise or in the cloud, such SAP-specific malware targets the areas in the model customers are responsible for.

Beyond SAP’s own responsibilities to secure our products and cloud solutions, we consider it important to support customers to run securely, as well. SAP Solution Manager, Cloud ALM, and solutions in the GRC suite support customers to configure their landscapes correctly.

When it comes to threat detection and integration of SAP security events into customers’ detection and incident response processes, there are several options available. SAP offers Enterprise Threat Detection, while several partners offer solutions on the SAP Store. Many customers don’t have SAP expertise on their cybersecurity teams. These offerings help bridge gaps between cybersecurity and SAP teams with security alerts specific to SAP solutions that can be investigated and addressed together.

 

Managing Security Incidents

Of course, we hope that it never happens. But despite your efforts to securely configure the landscape and address threats before they become a problem, you may encounter security alerts that indicate a security incident is in progress or has occurred. If SAP teams notice it first, they should alert their cybersecurity counterparts. If the security incident response team raised the incident ticket, they should involve their SAP team colleagues to triage and respond to the incident. Customers should report security issues to SAP. However, given the constraints mentioned before, there are limits potentially to what our security incident response team can do.

The incident may exceed the expertise of your in-house teams and require specialist outside help. Onapsis Research Labs provides direct customer support to customers going through an SAP security incident. Through this service, customers can get assistance from experienced professionals that have worked through many such incidents before. They conduct threat intelligence and are familiar with tactics and techniques used by adversaries targeting SAP systems. The service offering is listed on the SAP Store and you can find more information as well in Onapsis’s blog. 

This service offering is an important addition to support customers in their SAP security programs across all the functions of the NIST Cyber Security Framework.

 

More Information

There are several previous blogs that cover the theme of business and cyber resilience, and building bridges between customer SAP teams and cyber security teams. Please find their links below:

  • Cyber Resilience and Cloud Transformation: Resolving the ERP Security Hamster Wheel
  • “What Will SAP Basis Administrators Do Once We Move to RISE?” – Thoughts Following SAPinsider 2024
  • How SAP Security Teams Can Help Cyber Teams Towards Cyber Resilience

entry.title: Re: DTW Updates to BP House Bank
entry.content: hola Salo, encontraste la solución para estos mensajes de error?

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
Interesting read.. though I can't really agree with some of your examples. The main issue with "gt_vbak" is not the HN part for me, it's the 'vbak' part that is misleading. 

So if you're comparing  'gt_orders' with 'orders', then it comes down to the developer guidelines and naming conventions that are agreed upon (or imposed). As long as it is consistent. But I'd say that for an inconsistent developer the result will be inconsistent. HN or not. 

And it's not the HN that misleads or lies, it's the developer writing the code! Using meaningful names wouldn't lie or mislead I guess? But again, in the example I used above, it's the 'vbak' part that is misleading.. the fact that it is a global variable might be interesting to know.

So, yes, guidelines! Code review. Adapted ATC checks (that preferably make sure you cannot transport stuff that doesn't correspond to the guidelines)

entry.title: SAP instance reached state SHUTDOWN after having state STARTING
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks in advance, I am going to fresh installation of SAP ECC6 EHP8, using software provision manager SWPM10SP42.

OS = SUSE 15

Kernel = 7.53

database=Oracle 12.2

In executing phase getting error :

An error occurred while processing option SAP Business Suite 7i 2016 > EHP8 for SAP ERP 6.0 ABAP > Oracle > Installation > Application Server ABAP > Standard System > Standard System (Last error reported by the step: Instance CYQ/D00 reached state SHUTDOWN after having state STARTING. Giving up). You can now:

I have checked the profile multiple times, all parameters are seem ok.

Please see the attached error log files.

entry.title: Re: Managing Threats and Security Incidents for SAP Systems
entry.content: Having experienced incidents and worked with many organization who have experienced incidents I can say that they can happen to any organization, and when they do happen having the right people with the relevant experience being part of the response team is critical.

entry.title: Re: Developer in 2024 : which is the best way ?
entry.content: Hi Christophe, you will find details on the different approaches in the second learning offering Margit shared in her answer. If you need a deep dive into RAP, look for the tutorials on help.sap.com or to the S4D437 training (new version coming soon). BTW: RAP is the same on all platforms, whether you develop inside the S/4-system or on SAP BTP, ABAP Environment (side-by-side).

Joe

entry.title: Re: Unable to Upload Meta-data button is disabled please...
entry.content: Hi, I need your assistance with an issue I'm facing regarding the following endpoint: POST 'https://<IAS>/oauth2/token' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \ -H 'Authorization: Basic <token>' \ -d '{ "password": "<IASPassword>", "username": "<IASUsername>", "grant_type": "password" }' I am using my SAP SuccessFactors credentials instead of IAS credentials, as my SAP SuccessFactors credentials and IAS credentials are different. However, I am encountering the following error: { "error": "invalid_grant", "error_description": "User authentication failed." } Here’s the corrected and refined version of your sentence: "Hi, I need your assistance with an issue I'm facing regarding the following endpoint: vbnet Copy code POST 'https://<IAS>/oauth2/token' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \ -H 'Authorization: Basic <token>' \ -d '{ "password": "<IASPassword>", "username": "<IASUsername>", "grant_type": "password" }' I am using my SAP SuccessFactors credentials instead of IAS credentials, as my SAP SuccessFactors credentials and IAS credentials are different. However, I am encountering the following error: json Copy code { "error": "invalid_grant", "error_description": "User authentication failed." } Please help me resolve this issue so I can proceed further to obtain the SAML assertion required to use the ODATA APIs.

entry.title: What's New: Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis
entry.content:
It can be frustrating when your accounts payable department comes to you wondering whether something's wrong with a withholding tax posting, and it can be even worse if such an inquiry comes from a customer. This can mean extensive checking and cross-referencing, costing you both time and money.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, help is on the way in the form of the Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis app. This app takes you from start to finish to answer one question: How was withholding tax calculated for this transaction? 

Want to know more about what's coming? Head on over to the What's New Viewer and search for Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis--or just click here to head to the What's New entry for it. 

And while you're there, have a look at the how-to video in the what's new topic to get a quick overview on how you'll benefit from the app. And if you have some questions that aren't answered by the what's new topic (or the documentation preview that's linked from there), go ahead and leave a comment using the comments feature. 

One quick note: In this initial release, this app is released for Germany. Stay tuned!

Mridul Shukla

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite Custom UDF in Message Mapping
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check the following code:

import com.sap.it.api.mapping.*

def void getProperty(String[] propertyName, Output output, MappingContext context) {
    def propertyValue = context.getProperty(propertyName[0])
    if (propertyValue) {
        def values = propertyValue.split(",")
        values.each { value ->
            output.addValue(value.trim())
        }
    } else {
        output.addValue("")
    }
}

entry.title: Re: Usage Statistics of Table and Canvas Reports to Plan Transition to Stories
entry.content:
SAP said

Info for Customer

Proposed Solution:

Hi Phoebe,

Thnak you for your patience!
As per your query regarding the lesser data in the report which you are pulling from the site statics under admin for 2023 year as compare to the year 2024.  In order to investigate the issue I have discussed your issue internally and found that the data which is available under the admin which you are pulling is an audit data. So, the data which is available over there will going to populate in the report which is an expected behavior of audit data.
What data it generate it does there is nothing we can do from our side because its an audit data. 

Thank you for understanding!

As there is nothing pending from my side, i am moving the case status to “Resolved State” as per our customer support process guidelines. 

 

I would recommend you save your 2024 data before they disappeared in the Site Statistics audit report.

entry.title: Accrual/Deferral TPM44
entry.content:
Good afternoon, colleagues:

I have a problem with transaction TPM44, TRM. My client needs a solution to prevent accruals from being posted for the full month in cases where the collateral or financing does not start on day 1. The solution should allow automatically offsetting the payment installment with the interest and the accrual. This customer triggers accruals at the end of each month.

Here is an example:


Commissions for this guarantee are charged on the 17th of the month.

  • On 17.03.2024, 1162.11 EUR is charged.
  • On 30.03.2024, the 14 days of March are accrued for the amount of 180.77 EUR.
  • On 30.04.2024, the entire month of April is accrued for the amount of 387.37 EUR.
  • On 30.05.2024, the entire month of May is accrued for the amount of 387.37 EUR.
  • On 17.06.2024, the next quarter of the guarantee risk commission is charged.

In June, 387.37 EUR is accrued corresponding to:

  • 16 days of the accrual remaining from the charge in March, amounting to 206.60 EUR.
  • 14 days of the accrual of the new charge in June, amounting to 180.77 EUR.

In order to compensate the account, the system should “split” the June accrual so that when charging the second-quarter commission, the first-quarter commission is compensated. The way it currently works, this type of guarantee could not be offset until it is finalized.

Thank you for your help and time.

#tpm44, #trm

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Time-series Based Supply planning with Optimizer
entry.content: Hi Hendrik, all the productions seem to be adjusted. So the optimizer can only choose which demands it satisfies. Due to the coverage-constraint the optimizer will probably choose to satisfy the demands at the end of the planning horizon. Is this the case?

entry.title: Successfactors login issue - looping
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing an issue with my SF. When I'm trying to login, after the identification the page gets reloading with 4 different URLs and it's going over and over without actually logging in to the site.

Did anyone face the same issue before? What is the solution for this?

 

Thanks,

Peter

entry.title: Re: sp_move_file does not find the file with "č" character in filename
entry.content:
Well, according to the docs, xp_read_file() and xp_write_file() do treat the filename as LONG VARCHAR parameters, not as LONG NVARCHAR, so that at least may make a difference.

> Actually, the strings used in the database are all NVARCHAR.
What do you mean by that? Do you only use NVARCHAR strings, or do you use CHAR collation with UCA encoding, so both CHAR and NCHAR data are encoded with UTF-8? What does the following return?
select db_property('Collation'), db_property('CharSet'), db_property('NCharCollation'), db_property('NcharCharSet')

entry.title: Re: Flow Builder Plus - benefits, basic details, configurations, etc
entry.content: Hello, all the links to KBAs and SAP Notes are pointing to SAP Internal systems and are not accessible by non-SAP Employees.

entry.title: Re: Ways to add Search Helps in SAP UI5 Application
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing svelagala.

As a starting point it was good. 🙂

I needed to add a Search help to a standard field in a standard Fiori app and it was a painfull way starting with cl_fis_shlp_annotation=>create. I had to create the Entity, the EntitySet and make the selection myself in the get_entityset but with implicit extensions it was all possible. And I can proudly report: It works! 🤘

entry.title: FIORI Cache Maintenance
entry.content:
Clear Cache Effectively in Fiori App Development

Cache management is an essential part of Fiori app development. It is crucial to follow these steps to ensure all critical changes are verified and avoid issues arising from stale data across different modules. Below is a structured guide to clear caches effectively in your SAP environment.

1. Clear the Server HTTP Cache

  • Go to transaction SMICM
  • Navigate to Goto → HTTP Plugin → Server Cache.
  • Select Invalidate Locally and Invalidate Globally.

2. Clear Metadata Cache

  • On the Gateway: Run transaction /n/IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP to clear OData cache.

  • On the Backend and Gateway: Run transaction /n/IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP for additional OData cache clearing.

3. Synchronize Chip Cache

  • Run the report /UI2/CHIP_SYNCHRONIZE_CACHE using transaction SE38. 
  • Execute the reports /UI2/DELETE_CACHE_AFTER_IMP and /UI2/DELETE_CACHE via SE38.

4. Run Calculation of SAPUI5 Application Index

  • Execute the report /UI5/APP_INDEX_CALCULATE using transaction SE38. You can select different options here: Full calculation (very time consuming), calculation by period of time or calculation only for required SAPUI5 Repository.

5. Clear Local Browser Cache

  • Manually clear the browser cache to prevent loading outdated views and resources, or utilize an integrated cache buster solution.

6. Clear UI2 Cache

  • Run report /UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES using SE38. This step is essential, especially for verifying translations of tiles, groups, and catalogs in Launchpad admin.

---

P.S. Here's your turbo-charged guide for those consultants who sprint through SAP like pros, ready to zap stale data faster than you can say "cache cleared." Because, let's face it, yesterday’s data is so last week! 🏃‍💨📈

  1. SMICM: Goto → HTTP Plugin → Server Cache → Invalidate Locally / Globally
  2. /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP
  3. /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP
  4. SE38: Run /UI2/CHIP_SYNCHRONIZE_CACHE
  5. SE38: Run /UI2/DELETE_CACHE_AFTER_IMP
  6. SE38: Run /UI2/DELETE_CACHE
  7. SE38: Run /UI5/APP_INDEX_CALCULATE
  8. Clear local browser cache manually or use a cache buster
  9. SE38: Run /UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES

Ensure these cache maintenance techniques are applied whenever necessary to maintain effective application performance and data integrity. Following these steps will save time and prevent potential issues from arising in Fiori app development.

entry.title: Text field from Sales order to finance posting
entry.content:
which text fields can be used for information to be transfered from a sales order to finance?

at the moment just the field assignment in FI can be seen, that corresponds to the customer reference in the sales order.

We need the text field to be available in the FI Apps like Manage Customer Line Items, Post Incoming Payments (Open AP/AR Items), etc.

entry.title: Re: Parallel workflow approval in SAP Success Factors
entry.content:
In the workflow actions option there is an action called 'Multi User Approval' you can use that, for this specific case in that case all the different departments can approve the task on there own convenience not after one user does and then other.

Another possible option is using the action 'custom multi user action' that enable multiple users to approve a case  potentially from different people in different departments, before a case can move to the next status, and before other actions will execute in the current status.


https://help.sap.com/docs/Advanced_Workflow/b7cb13f8b6a04157a722ad9ddfde4a28/72abb4a57c231014a804993ce4041860.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/Advanced_Workflow/b7cb13f8b6a04157a722ad9ddfde4a28/72a3a9dd7c231014a804993ce4041860.html

entry.title: Re: Question about replication flow in initial mode with...
entry.content: I use ODP. Loading from BW/4. Did not see such issue. Check please if you have all the data. 1:1 to CDS. I bet yes. This is just a monitoring issue, where the flow thinks it is in delta mode. I guess a bug

entry.title: Re: Alerta ao Migrar tabela J_1BTAXCODEV
entry.content:
Resulação do problema. Alterar a sequência de acesso da condição de IBRR para BCO2.

Eu tive esse problema na ICBS e IPBS.

entry.title: Re: Importing Scope from the Digital Discovery Assessment into SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Fabian!

Thank you for great content of your blogs!

I have a question concerning baseline accelerator and some warnings that I get.
By every upload I get a warning:

"The content version of the uploaded JSON scope file does not match with the content version of the project. The mismatch may affect the availability of bundles and scenarios in your JSON file. You can see the details in the list of rejected scenarios. Once the import is completed, please view the imported scope in My Selected Scope."

My CBC is on 2409, the Content and the target system is on 2408, no other skope items are defined.

In JSON Test File: ContentVersion 2408, 2 Countries and 2 scope items

Although there are only 2 countries and 2 scope items and CBC give me next worning:

"The number of countries/regions in your scope exceeds the recommended limit of five. The number of scenarios in your scope exceeds the threshold. The import may take some time. Do you really want to proceed?"

 

Is this normal behavior and CBC need always add baseline accelerators scope items?
How should I understand 1st warning?

Thank you in advance for your help!

 

BR
Tomasz

entry.title: SAP TM Private cloud implementation - Query
entry.content:
Hi TM experts,

We have S/4 HANA on premise 2021 version and currently we are using on premise TMS solution which integrates with Blue Yonder. Now, the client wants to implement SAP TM - Private cloud edition and replace Blue Yonder. 

I have the below queries:

1. What data transfer technique will be used to exchange the data between S/4 on premise and TM private cloud (Outbound and Inbound, both)? Will it be IDoc or Proxies and why? What is the SAPs recommendation?

2. Based on the above data transfer technique used, how middleware like Mulesoft & Bizlink will play a role in data transfer?

Urgent response awaited! Thank you in advance!

SAP Transportation Management

How can do it please?

entry.content:
Hi TM experts,

We have S/4 HANA on premise 2021 version and currently we are using on premise TMS solution which integrates with Blue Yonder. Now, the client wants to implement SAP TM - Private cloud edition and replace Blue Yonder. 

I have the below queries:

1. What data transfer technique will be used to exchange the data between S/4 on premise and TM private cloud (Outbound and Inbound, both)? Will it be IDoc or Proxies and why? What is the SAPs recommendation?

2. Based on the above data transfer technique used, how middleware like Mulesoft & Bizlink will play a role in data transfer?

Urgent response awaited! Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: PPOME task for creating position id is a direct maste...

I added Org unit with PPOME T-code and the Positions were created with PO13 so that it allowed me to use external allowed numbering, which I matched with the Agent id moved in the TR from DEV.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAC connection with S4 Private Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @AJ02,

I don't know the error message, but maybe the following white paper helps you in solving your issue
Technical Integration Guide by IBsolution and SAP SE - Connect SAP S/4HANA On-Premise to SAP Analytics Cloud Planning 

It explains how you need to configure SAP Analytics Cloud, the Cloud Connector, and S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, as well as the needed prerequisites and authorizations.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: First Date field not visible in Mass changes of Sales documents Fiori App
entry.content:
I added First date in the CDS view of Mass change of sales document items  Fiori App and it is updated in OData as well. However, when I try to add it in the SAP Fiori App screen it is not visible  

 

Please let  me know if anyone if aware of this issue and any small help is really appreciated

entry.title: How to automatically add a text in transaction ME21N -> Texts -> Terms of Delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to automatically add a predefined text for the delivery terms when creating a custom purchase order of type ZXX in SAP. How can I configure this in SAP so that a predefined text automatically fills the 'Terms of Delivery' text field every time a PO is created?

entry.title: Re: Brownfield Conversion from EHS Product Safety (ECC) t...
entry.content:
Hello

reading between the lines you will use this approach

You have an ECC landscape (DEV(QUAL/PROD); then you will prepare a new landscape DEV/QUAL/PROD for S/4 HANA

Step 1. UNDERSTAND THE SIMPLIFICAITON LIST FOR EHS CLASSIC AND S/4

Step 2: then the problem with "Cover Sheet" etc. is "peanuts" as you must first migrate the data (there is much more to look at)

Step 3; to do the job... find a service provider which will help you

I can not list here all the topics you have to "pay attention" on. The list is to huge

I wish you success

C.B.

entry.title: Voucher for certification exam
entry.content:
Hello!
I am a professor at a university in Chile. Last year I participated in the Strategic & Management Consultant SAP S/4HANA (TS410) program at Brandenburg University and passed that program.

I would like to know how I can obtain student vouchers to attend a certification exam, since I failed my first attempt.

I look forward to your comments.

Best regards,

Susana

Certification Careers

entry.title: Few currency codes unavailable
entry.content:
The below currency codes which are present in the ISO list, are not available for input in the system.

ISO CodeLong TextShort text
CYPCyprus PoundCyprus Pound
EEKEstonian KroneKrona
LTLLithuanian LitaLita
LVLLatvian LatLat
MTLMaltese LiraLira
SKKSlovakian KronaKrona

There are few currencies which have a different currency code in our system from ISO list:

SAP CurrencyISO CodeLong Text
AFNAFAAfghani (Old)
RSDCSDSerbian Dinar
MRUMROMauritanian Ouguiya
RONROLRomanian Leu
SLESLLSierra Leone Leone
STNSTDSao Tome / Principe Dobra
TMTTMMTurkmenistani Manat
TRYTRLTurkish Lira (Old)
VEFVEBVenezuelan Bolivar

 

 

#SAPS4HANAPublicCloud #2408

entry.title: Periodificaciones TPM44 TRM
entry.content:
Buenas tardes, compañeros:

Tengo un problema con la transacción TPM44, TRM. Mi cliente necesita una solución para evitar que las periodificaciones se registren por el mes completo en aquellos casos en los que el aval o el financiamiento no comiencen el día 1. La solución debería permitir compensar automáticamente la cuota de pago de  los intereses, con el saldo de la periodificación. Este cliente ejecuta las periodificaciones al final de cada mes.

Aquí hay un ejemplo:

Los intereses de esta garantía se cargan el día 17 de cada mes.

  • El 17.03.2024 se carga  1162,11 EUR.
  • El 30.03.2024 se periodifican los 14 días de marzo por un importe de 180,77 EUR.
  • El 30.04.2024 se periodifican el mes completo de abril por un importe de 387,37 EUR.
  • El 30.05.2024 se periodifican el mes completo de mayo por un importe de 387,37 EUR.
  • El 17.06.2024 se carga la siguiente trimestre de la comisión de riesgo del aval.

En junio se periodifica 387,37 eur correspondientes a :

  • 16 días de la periodificación que queda pendiente del cargo en marzo por importe de 206,60 eur.
  • 14 días de la periodificación del nuevo cargo de junio por importe de 180,77 eur.

Para poder ir compensando la cuenta, el sistema debería "dividir" la periodificación de junio de forma que al cargar la comisión del segundo trimestre quede compensada la del primer trimestre. De la forma en la que funciona actualmente, este tipo de avales no se podrían compensar hasta que finalicen.

Gracias por vuestra ayuda y tiempo.

#tpm44, #trm

entry.title: EC Payroll off cycle before the last pay date (MX)
entry.content:
Hi team,

It is possible payroll off-cycle with a pay date before the last ordinary payroll.

Example:

The last payroll pay date is december 13, but we want to run off cycle payroll for a special bonus on december 11.

we tried to do that but the calculation gave us the following validation:

Current payroll payment date 11.12.2024 less than last payroll payment date 13.12.2024

Best regards. HCM Payroll Mexico

Now to correct the SES Qty , we are unable revoke the SES.

What is ideal to do in this situation.

--

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Can we get the data in table format of the chart in s...
entry.content:
Hi @Nagavalli_BN ,

In chart widget we can enable Data Analyzer, once it is enabled in Chart widget action icon, we can see an option Data analyzer, if we click on it chart will be converted to Table, and we can download the file.

Regards,

Subbu

entry.title: Retrieving Sorted Data from CHAR Field with Numeric Value (SAP Basis 7.50+, S/4)
entry.content:
Introduction:

Let me share a curious situation I recently ran into. Imagine you need to fetch the maximum value from a table field. Easy peasy, right? Just use ORDER BY in your SQL SELECT statement and you're golden. Well, not so fast. That works perfectly when the field is of type NUMC, Integer, or Float. But what happens if the field is of type CHAR, even though it's storing numerical data?

I tried using ORDER BY DESCENDING on such a CHAR field. It worked fine for single-digit numbers (0-9). But as soon as it encountered two-digit numbers like 10, the magic broke. Suddenly, 1 > 10 (in SQL’s eyes). Oof! Frustrating, isn’t it?

So, what now?

Idea 1: Fetch All the Data

We could just pull all the records into a temporary internal table, sort them locally, and pick the first one. Problem solved, right? Well, not really. Why? Because:

1. We are fetching a ton of data we don’t even need.
2. If the table’s transactional data is massive, you’re looking at a potential performance bottleneck. Nobody wants their program slowing to a crawl!

Idea 2: Design Change

Should we redesign the field type? Or even nudge SAP to change it for us? Sure, that’s an option. But do we have that kind of time and is it not going to cause in any other problem? Probably not.

CAST to the Rescue! Now, this is where things get exciting! If you’re using ABAP release 7.50+ or S/4HANA, SAP has handed us a secret weapon: the CAST syntax. It’s like a Swiss Army knife for your SELECT statements. Here’s the game plan:

  1. First, we use CAST to convert the CHAR field into NUMC.

  2. Then, like a magic trick with an encore, we chain another CAST to transform NUMC into Integer (or INT8).

Yes, you heard me right—chaining CAST statements is your golden ticket! It’s like building a performance-boosting machine one layer at a time. Let’s dive into the action.

For example, I needed to select the max value from Field TEXT3 of table VTTK (Shipment Header). This field is CHAR40. So I constructed the Select statement with Open SQL expressions and by use of CAST as per below.  Here lv_text3 is the maximum value stored in a numerical format.

 

             SELECT
             FROM vttk
             FIELDS CAST( CAST( text3  AS NUMC( 40 ) ) AS INT8 ) AS text3
             WHERE text3 NE @space
             ORDER BY text3 DESCENDING
             INTO @DATA(lv_text3) UP TO 1 ROWS.
             ENDSELECT.
*OR
             SELECT
             FROM vttk
             FIELDS MAX( CAST( CAST( text3  AS NUMC( 40 ) ) AS INT8 ) ) AS text3
             WHERE text3 NE @space
             INTO @DATA(lv_text3).

 

And just like that, we have turned a tricky problem into a smooth, high-performance solution. No unnecessary data fetching. No frustrating bottlenecks. Just elegant, efficient ABAP magic. 

This ends the functionality.

Appreciate you stopping by and reading the article! Your comments, suggestions, and shares are greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: "Service does not exist" while creating IAM app in ec...
entry.content:
Hi @kaidehmann
I have the same issue 
I try to use the "Browse" option, or code completion (on Windows: enter Z and press Ctrl + Space). and my service is not there 
I did all steps as show in SAP Learning exercice "Create an SAP Fiori App and Deploy it to SAP BTP, ABAP Environment"
in deploy SAP Fiori Generator - in "Data Source and Service Selection" my service is in the list but in
Eclipse in identify and access management - IAM Apps when i try to create a new service my service is not in the list
Somebody find any way to fix that?

entry.title: Held purchase requisitions need to be seen throug trx. MD04
entry.content:
The customer we are working with at the moment states that, in transaction MD04, they used to be able to see and account for held Purchase Requisitions before a recent upgrade to EhP7. They now want this functionality to be available again.

We believe they might have had a Z development, but at the same time, they want to work in the most standard way possible.

 

Is there a standard way to make held PRs available in transaction MD04?

 

Thank you very much for your help!

Simplification List for SAP GTS, edition for SAP HANA

Upgrade Guide for SAP GTS, edition for SAP HANA

Configuration Guide for SAP GTS, edition for SAP HANA

Gabriel

entry.title: Inside the December Edition - Thank you for Making This Year Unforgettable!
entry.content:
The December Edition of the SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter is here! Warm wishes for a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year - let's step into 2025 stronger and more connected than ever.

SAP Energy and Utilities Team 

Dear Oil, Gas, and Energy Community,

The end of the year is approaching fast, providing an opportunity to recall memorable moments we shared and incredible milestones reached in 2024.

This year, we've traveled to Seville, Houston, Abu Dhabi, Porto, Monaco, Orlando, Barcelona, and many other fascinating destinations to get to know you personally, connect, exchange ideas, and learn from one another. Whether in person or online, we never miss an opportunity to engage.

And we’re already gearing up for our next big event: Get ready to discover the latest trends and solutions at the next annual SAP Energy and Utilities Conference. Hope to see you in Rotterdam from April 8-10, 2025!

In 2024, we introduced a new webinar format – SAP Espresso Sessions to keep you up to date on important industry topics and address your queries.

Throughout the year, our journey has been a whirlwind of innovation, collaboration, and discovery—all captured in our annual highlight video. We invite you to take a few minutes to revisit these wonderful experiences shared across the globe.

As 2025 approaches, our focus remains on SAP innovations. Harnessing the potential of Artificial intelligence, we are thrilled to pursue transformative solutions powered by AI, paving the way to the future!

This year, we proudly launched the SAP Field Logistics solution, a groundbreaking tool that empowers field workers to efficiently manage orders, schedules, deliveries, and services.

Along with our partner Wipro, we developed the SAP STO 360 end-to-end solution on SAP BTP. This innovation has redefined the way we manage maintenance shutdowns for plants, in Oil, Gas, and Energy, ensuring we stay ahead of evolving industry needs.

Would you like to know more about our customers? In this edition, read stories from Halliburton, Woodside Energy, Crescent Petroleum, SATORP, Falcon Oilfield Fuels, Intergaz LTD, Squadron Energy, KazMunayGas, SK C&C, and many more.

We are entering 2025 equipped with new technologies, cutting-edge innovations, and what’s most important great people next to us, whether it is our family, friends, or helpful colleagues.

The SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Team wishes you and your dear ones a Happy Holiday season and a peaceful and prosperous New Year!

 

Read the full version of the SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter and subscribe to receive the next issue directly in your e-mail box.

entry.title: How to Set Up a Realistic SAP S/4HANA Practice Environment?
entry.content: What are the best ways to set up a realistic SAP S/4HANA test environment for learning? I want to work hands-on with tools like SAP Process Builder, ABAP, and cloud deployment in a way that mirrors real-world usage. Are there affordable sandbox or REALISTIC trial options available for this (including SAP Joule and all the products that are part of a full SAP license)?

entry.title: Re: show only one value which is post on latest
entry.content:
it would be better if you would provide how you are dealing with AT ENDAT. But as I can see from you screenshot BUDAT at the beggingng of the line, and matnr and werks is inside the line. It is NOT an option for AT ENDAT - your components should be one by one. AT ENDAT compare not only field itself but from the begining to the field. Below I have provided some snippet how to deal with AT ENDAT. But please check and inform whether it is clear.

Then: my advise to use either (depeding on the system version and context) SORTED table or GROUP BY. I also have provided a snippet. full code is here. Thank you for your answer.

Sorry for 2 hundred lines, but it is the best way how I could explain what I mean.

 

 

 

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS main.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_forecast
            , budat TYPE sydatum
            , timestamp TYPE timestamp
            , vtstamp TYPE timestamp
            , racct TYPE char10
            , matnr TYPE matnr18
            , werks TYPE werks_d
            , lbkum TYPE umlme
            , msl TYPE umlme
            , poper TYPE char2
            , gjahr TYPE char4
          , END OF ts_forecast
          , tt_forecast TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_forecast WITH EMPTY KEY
          .

    DATA mt_some_forecast TYPE tt_forecast.

    METHODS _do_load_prefill.


    METHODS _read_max_by_at_new.
    METHODS _read_max_by_srt.
    METHODS _read_max_group_by.
    METHODS _read_max_group_by_no_membr.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.



    _do_load_prefill( ).


    _read_max_by_at_new( ).
    _read_max_by_srt( ).
    _read_max_group_by( ).
    _read_max_group_by_no_membr( ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _read_max_by_at_new.

    DATA lt_some_forecast_init TYPE tt_forecast.
    DATA lt_tab_with_max_date TYPE tt_forecast.

    DATA lv_combination_is_new TYPE abap_bool.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_forecast> TYPE ts_forecast.
    lt_some_forecast_init = mt_some_forecast. " just to to change initial tab for other approaches

    SORT lt_some_forecast_init BY matnr werks  " you need to do sorting
                               budat DESCENDING
                               .


    "" this WILL NOT GROUP CORRECTLY because budat is differ and it is on the LEFR from your component MATNR
    "" NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~
    LOOP AT lt_some_forecast_init ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast>.
      lv_combination_is_new = abap_false.
      AT NEW matnr .
        lv_combination_is_new = abap_true.
      ENDAT.
      AT NEW werks .
        lv_combination_is_new = abap_true.
      ENDAT.

      IF lv_combination_is_new EQ abap_true.
        APPEND <fs_some_forecast> TO lt_tab_with_max_date.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    "" NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~ NO GROUP ~~~~~
    """""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""'
    """""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""'
    """""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""'

    """ but here
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_key_at_begin_of_line
         , matnr TYPE matnr18 " what is in the AT_ENDAT - should be at the BEGGING
        , werks TYPE werks_d  " what is in the AT_ENDAT - should be at the BEGGING
        , budat TYPE sydatum
        , timestamp TYPE timestamp
        , vtstamp TYPE timestamp
        , racct TYPE char10
        , lbkum TYPE umlme
        , msl TYPE umlme
        , poper TYPE char2
        , gjahr TYPE char4
      , END OF ts_key_at_begin_of_line
      , tt_key_at_begin_of_line TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_key_at_begin_of_line WITH EMPTY KEY
      .

    DATA lt_key_at_begin_of_line  TYPE tt_key_at_begin_of_line .
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_key_at_begin> TYPE ts_key_at_begin_of_line .
    DATA lt_tab_with_max_date2 TYPE tt_forecast.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING lt_some_forecast_init TO lt_key_at_begin_of_line.
    CLEAR lt_tab_with_max_date2.

    LOOP AT lt_key_at_begin_of_line ASSIGNING <fs_key_at_begin>.
      lv_combination_is_new = abap_false.
      AT NEW matnr .
        lv_combination_is_new = abap_true.
      ENDAT.
      AT NEW werks .
        lv_combination_is_new = abap_true.
      ENDAT.

      IF lv_combination_is_new EQ abap_true.
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_tab_with_max_date2 ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast>.
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING <fs_key_at_begin> TO <fs_some_forecast>.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    "

    BREAK-POINT.
    " please compare lt_tab_with_max_date2 and lt_tab_with_max_date

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _read_max_by_srt.
    DATA lt_tab_with_max_date TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ts_forecast WITH UNIQUE KEY matnr werks.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_forecast> TYPE ts_forecast.

    SORT mt_some_forecast BY matnr werks
                             budat DESCENDING.

    LOOP AT mt_some_forecast ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast>.
      INSERT <fs_some_forecast> INTO TABLE lt_tab_with_max_date.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _read_max_group_by.
    DATA lt_tab_with_max_date TYPE tt_forecast.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_forecast> TYPE ts_forecast.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_with_max_date> TYPE ts_forecast.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_grp> TYPE ts_forecast.

    SORT mt_some_forecast BY matnr werks
                             budat DESCENDING.

    " this is a quite overhead approach in your case

    LOOP AT mt_some_forecast ASSIGNING <fs_some_grp> " it is group- pseudo-oboject - not LINE, but GROUP
          GROUP BY ( key1 = <fs_some_grp>-matnr  key2 = <fs_some_grp>-werks ). " you are providing keys

     " LOOP AT GROUP <fs_some_grp> ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast>. " here we get LINE from GROUP
        READ TABLE mt_some_forecast ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast>
            WITH KEY matnr = <fs_some_grp>-matnr
                     werks = <fs_some_grp>-werks.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
          APPEND <fs_some_forecast> TO lt_tab_with_max_date.
        ENDIF.
      "ENDLOOP.

    ENDLOOP.


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _read_max_group_by_no_membr.
    DATA lt_tab_with_max_date TYPE tt_forecast.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_key_line
           , matnr TYPE matnr18
            , werks TYPE werks_d
        , END OF ts_key_line
        .
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_forecast> TYPE ts_forecast.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_with_max_date> TYPE ts_forecast.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_some_grp> TYPE ts_forecast.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_key_line> TYPE ts_key_line.

    CLEAR lt_tab_with_max_date.

    SORT mt_some_forecast BY matnr werks
                             budat DESCENDING.

    """ IF need oly keys - you can use WITHOUT MEMBERS

    LOOP AT mt_some_forecast ASSIGNING <fs_some_grp> " it is group- pseudo-oboject - not LINE, but GROUP
          GROUP BY ( matnr = <fs_some_grp>-matnr werks = <fs_some_grp>-werks )  " you are providing keys
          WITHOUT MEMBERS " and here we get only keys without addition fields
      ASSIGNING <fs_key_line>. " in case without membes we could not LOOP at GROUP

      READ TABLE mt_some_forecast ASSIGNING <fs_with_max_date>
            WITH KEY matnr = <fs_key_line>-matnr
                     werks = <fs_key_line>-werks.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_tab_with_max_date ASSIGNING <fs_some_forecast> .
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING <fs_with_max_date> TO <fs_some_forecast>.
      ENDIF.


    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _do_load_prefill.
    DATA lv_ts TYPE timestamp.
    GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_ts.

    mt_some_forecast = VALUE #(
     ( budat = sy-datum - 20 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'D001' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )

     ( budat = sy-datum - 20 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'DK02' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )

     ( budat = sy-datum - 20 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'D001' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )

     ( budat = sy-datum - 30 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'DK02' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )

     ( budat = sy-datum - 30 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'D001' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )

     ( budat = sy-datum - 30 timestamp = lv_ts vtstamp = lv_ts
      racct = '22000' matnr = '2400011' werks = 'D001' lbkum = 10 msl = 3 poper = 4 gjahr = 2025 )
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  NEW lcl_app( )->main( ).

entry.title: Re: Webhooks in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content: Great Blog !!

entry.title: Re: Reset (planned) picking status when cancelling WT for PDO
entry.content: Hello Tim, is this issue resolved ? I am facing same issue

entry.title: Re: Employee clocking in at 11:30PM for a midnight shift
entry.content: @Volker_Ruof I had heard that was the case, but was hoping it wasn't true! You are a legend in the SF Time Community and will be greatly missed. Enjoy your retirement!

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Yerevan
entry.content:
We are excited to invite you to the upcoming SAP Community Meetup, where you’ll hear the latest news and updates on local SAP initiatives and gain valuable insights from SAP experts and representatives of the academic community.

🗓 Event Details:

  • Date: 17 December 2024
  • Time: 17:00
  • Location: ASUE, Erasmus Hall (NOTE: Last minute changes of the hall may occur)
  • Audience: Open to anyone interested in SAP solutions and training opportunities in Armenia.

Registration: Preferably through this page or in case you are not a member of SAP Community you can also use our Eventbrite registration

What’s on the Agenda?

 

  • Overview of Upcoming Initiatives and free SAP Trainings in Armenia

    • Speakers:
      • Sos Khachikyan, Head of the Chair of Macroeconomics
      • Tsovinar Chugaszyan, SAP Business Technology Platform Product Manager

  • SAP in Armenia

    • Speaker: Valeri Sargsyan, SAP Armenia Country Manager

  • Presentations by Local Partners and Academic Community members

    • Insights from industry experts on real-world applications of SAP solutions.

  • Networking

    • Connect with SAP representatives, local partners, and fellow attendees to explore collaboration opportunities and future initiatives.

entry.title: CPI - Adapter RabbitMQ consumer Message - Wrong converstion to base64
entry.content:
I am using SAP CPI with a Groovy script, and when I post this message to the queue, it appears as Base64. However, in the logs of my middleware (SAP CPI), it is shown as a regular message.

When I post it to an online webhook, it also arrives normally as JSON.

Only in Adapter(AMQP)RabbitMQ does it arrive in Base64. The version is 4.0, and AMQP 1.0 is being used.

 

Message encode64 post in rabbit

eyJwYXR0ZXJuIjoidGVzdC5ldnQiLCJkYXRhIjp7IklEIjoiNjYzIiwiS2V5U0FQIjoiMDAwMSIsIlN0YXR1cyI6IlMiLCJNZW5zYWdlU0FQIjoiQ2xpZW50ZSAwMDAxIGNyZWF0ZWQgd2l0aCBzdWNjZXNzLiJ9fQ== 

Coding Groovy:

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) { 
def fixedBody = '{"pattern":"test.evt","data":{"ID":"663","KeySAP":"0001","Status":"S","MensageSAP":"Cliente 0001 created with success."}}'

message.setBody(fixedBody)


message.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/plain")
message.setHeader("CamelHttpCharacterEncoding", "UTF-8")

return message 
}

entry.title: Re: S4H to CPI. Need help
entry.content: This is crazy. Copy/paste worked. Thank you very much. Would you be able to tell me why the search drop down is not showing all options? Is this a config change somehwere?

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello!

Does anybody know when the T4A pdf form will be delivered for year 2024 in SAP HANA?

Thanks,
Nathalie Ann

entry.title: I am unable to retrieve sent offer letters in the new hire's profile
entry.content: I tried finding it via Job Requisitions as well as the new hire's profile. Any idea how to do this now?

entry.title: Re: S4H to CPI. Need help
entry.content: @Pravin28it looks like the search help is defaulted to display ports with type = '1' (tRFC), so I'm assuming you did the same as I, and setup a port with type = '7' (XML HTTP).  Note however, that you can adjust which search help is used by clicking the filter option.

entry.title: Province Field Blank in Canadian Compliance Forms
entry.content:
Compliance forms have been configured for the Onboarding process. 

The overrideFieldsMeta has been configured with the GLOBAL_FILTER_CONFIG for the Country and WorkState - Province in our case. 

EC Mini Master is being used. The Location object has been configured with every location being associated to a Province. 

The TD1 form triggers and the Province field is blank. Without this, the new hire is unable to submit the form. Also, the provincial form is not triggering.

Has anyone encountered this in their configuration? 

Any leads on what may be causing this? 

Your help and expertise is appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements CDS App Create button not visible in BAS & Fiori Luachpad
entry.content: Hi @MadhuS, Please share if service is V2 or V4 and Draft is enable if its V4 service...

entry.title: Re: Retrieving Sorted Data from CHAR Field with Numeric Value (SAP Basis 7.50+, S/4)
entry.content:
Thanks for the tip.

Generally speaking, it works like that in "all" programming languages. It happens for any comparison like in SORT and IF, e.g. the text '2' is greater than '10' because it compares one character at a time ('2' is greater than '1' and the comparison stops). If numeric types are used for the comparisons, the number 2 is lower than 10 as expected.

Concerning the database table columns containing numbers, it would be better to define columns of type numeric instead of character, of course. In your case, VTTK is very old design (custom fields were provided by SAP to contain anything). Hopefully, it's been a while that custom fields are to be appended to the tables when needed, any type can be chosen.

Note that CAST from CHAR to NUMC is not sufficient alone (you need a second CAST to a numeric type e.g. INT8 as you proposed), because the characters are copied without any transformation, the spaces and other non-numeric characters are kept as-is, etc.: "There is no special handling for the special target type for numeric text, NUMC, and for the date/time types DATS and TIMS. It is advisable to only convert valid values" (ABAP doc > CAST > Source Types CHAR, SSTRING > Character-like target type (e.g. NUMC)).

As you say, the construct "CAST( CAST( char AS NUMC( 40 ) ) AS numerictype )" is magic, also because NUMC is known to support only the digits 0 to 9 but CAST keeps the negative sign and the decimal point! "The validity of the content of the source field [of type NUMC] is not checked, which means that there can be decimal places." (ABAP doc > CAST > Source Type NUMC > Numeric target type).

Limitation: INT8 supports up to 19 digits (values from -9,223,372,036,854,775,808 to +9,223,372,036,854,775,807).

FLTP can be used instead of INT8 but the CAST keeps only 16 representative digits, so the comparison may be inexact if the number has 17 or more digits (e.g. '123456789012345670' and '123456789012345671' are both rounded to the same number 1.2345678901234566E+17).

Cast from NUMC to DECFLOAT34 is available since 7.54. It's the only solution to compare exactly numbers up to 34 digits.

NB: for NUMC, CAST of CDS Views has exactly the same behavior as ABAP SQL: ABAP Keyword Documentation > CAST > Character-Like Source Type.

entry.title: Re: Enable/Disable custom action button in SAP Fiori List...
entry.content:
Hi @tejaswikosanam 

You probabily need Feature control (Instant Feature), please have a look to below blog post contains what you needed...

action (feature : instance) <your action name> result [1] $self;

Also Implement the Get_instance_feature method to set button enable or disabled.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-add-feature-control-in-abap-restful-application-programming-model/ba-p/13546757 

Hope this helps...

 

Thanks-

entry.title: How to find the root cause of a high CPU utilization in SAP Focused RUN
entry.content:
Hi,

As part of monitoring, we could see serious CPU utilization spikes for our Live Production System.

Is there any possibility to find the details/cause for this spike at Focused Run Application?
Like who is the user or program/backend job which caused this high CPU spike.

How do we find true cause for this CPU spike in Focused Run?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to find the root cause of a high CPU utilization...
entry.content:
Hello Community,

To gather more data about these high usages, there are two applications in SAP Focused Run that can assist you:

  • System Analysis/Root Cause Analysis
    • This tool allows you to enable additional data collections for ABAP and HANA Systems (per system).
    • With this information is possible to analyze it further checking details of resource consumption during a high utilization time.
  • Real User Monitoring
    • This tool helps you identify end-user calls and communication flows.

For a better understanding of how to utilize these tools, please refer to the documentation above.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Your SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter Collection
entry.content:
Dear SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Community,

We are thrilled to announce that all our newsletter issues for 2024 and the upcoming 2025 will be conveniently available in one location. Whether you want to revisit a favorite issue or discover something new, these resources are easily accessible.

Your thoughts and feedback are incredibly valuable to us, and we would love to hear your opinion on the topics that you find interesting or helpful. 

Here’s how you can subscribe to the upcoming newsletter editions.

Thank you for being a part of our community. Happy reading!

Your SAP Energy and Utilities Team

2024

  • December
  • September
  • June
  • February

entry.title: "Number Range Status" in T code: SNUM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

Need your inputs for question on "Number Range Status" in T code: SNUM

(1) What is the use of Number Range status. I was thinking that is the last used number by system from the range, but when I check the number displayed in Number Range status, it does not show any results

(2) Can we say that based on Number range status, we can estimate how much series of numbers available for that number range? Example: 999 Numbers available (19999 - 19000)

Number RangeFrom NumberTo NumberNumber Range StatusAvailable Numbers
XX100001999919000999

(3) We have a situation where "Number Range Status" and "To Number", the difference is reducing each day, does it mean documents will not be posted, if the difference will be ZERO? 

(4) What can the actions be taken based on scenario mentioned in (3)? 

Thank you in advance for your response

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to use the OData Client Proxy in SAP S/4 HANA or How to use the OData Service Consumption Mo
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer,

I noticed that not all oData v2 services are supported when creating the service consumption model. I tried creating it in BTP ABAP trial for Sales order and it gave me this error.

I am using the EDMX file from this link: https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_SALES_ORDER_SRV_0001/overview

However, when I tried to create the model for Business Partner and Pricing Condition records, I didn't encounter this issue.

link for business partner: https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_BUSINESS_PARTNER_SRV/overview

link for pricing condition: https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_SLSPRCGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV_0001/overview

Could you have a look and it from your end? Thanks!

entry.title: "Revolutionizing SAP Recruitment with HireRig.AI"
entry.content:
The new white paper on HireRig.AI presents an innovative approach to SAP recruitment, addressing key challenges faced by businesses in adapting to rapid technological changes. By leveraging AI-driven, project-based hiring, HireRig.AI is set to transform traditional recruitment methods, eliminating biases, accelerating the hiring process, and ensuring a more accurate match between candidates’ skills and SAP project requirements.

Key insights from the white paper include:

  • How project-based hiring through HireRig.AI enhances recruitment efficiency and effectiveness.
  • Real-world case studies demonstrating the platform’s impact on reducing time-to-hire and mitigating project delays.
  • The cost-saving potential of flexible, scalable hiring models for SAP businesses. 

Link - https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7272695170360328192?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

For an in-depth exploration of how HireRig.AI can revolutionize your SAP talent acquisition strategy, I invite you to read the full white paper. Let’s collaborate to shape the future of SAP recruitment and drive innovation in the technical hiring ecosystem. Transform the Future of SAP Recruitment with HireRig.AI

entry.title: Re: Error during GRN posting
entry.content: combination assignment of material group and GL account is not possible

entry.title: Hi , SAP SD Rev.Rec (VF44) for Brazil
entry.content:
Hi ,

We run SAP SD revenue recognition VF44 . For Brazil VF44 is considering TAX like posting in account like :

'320002 '/ Brazil - Cofins Tax Offset

'320003' / Brazil - PIS Tax Offset

'320004' / Brazil - ISS Tax Offset

Any documentation or setup we have to do for Brazil ? Any help / feedback if welcome .

Thank you
